recipe.name: Eggplant and Minced Meat Casserole
instructions: Prepare the egg mixture: In a large bowl, whisk together 4 eggs, 1 teaspoon salt, 1 teaspoon black pepper, and 1 teaspoon oregano until well combined.
Add 1 cup heavy cream and 1 cup milk to the egg mixture and whisk to combine.
Stir in 1/2 cup all-purpose flour and 1 tablespoon baking powder, mixing until smooth and well blended. Set aside.
Cook the pasta: Bring a large saucepan of salted water to a boil. Add 8 ounces pasta and cook according to package instructions until al dente. Drain and transfer to a large bowl.
Prepare the meat sauce: Heat olive oil in a large frying pan over medium heat. Add the finely chopped white onion and sauté until translucent, about 3-4 minutes.
Add the grated garlic to the pan and cook for 1 minute until fragrant.
Add 18 ounces ground beef and pork mixture to the pan. Cook for 10-15 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the meat is browned and cooked through.
Season the meat with 1 teaspoon salt, 1 teaspoon black pepper, and 1 teaspoon oregano. Mix well and remove from heat.
Add the cooked meat mixture to the bowl with the pasta. Pour in 7 fluid ounces tomato sauce and mix everything together thoroughly.
Grate half of the cheese head and stir it into the pasta and meat mixture.
Grease a baking dish with olive oil. Pour the pasta and meat mixture into the dish and spread evenly.
Prepare the vegetables: Heat more olive oil in the same frying pan over medium heat. Add the coarsely chopped red onion and sauté until soft, about 3-4 minutes.
Add the eggplant strips to the pan and cook for 5 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Add the chopped bell peppers and finely chopped mushrooms to the pan. Stir to combine.
Add 14 ounces canned diced tomatoes to the vegetable mixture. Season with 1 teaspoon salt, 1 teaspoon black pepper, and 1 teaspoon oregano.
Let the vegetables simmer for about 10 minutes until tender and well combined, stirring occasionally.
Transfer the cooked vegetable mixture on top of the pasta and meat layer in the baking dish, spreading evenly.
Pour the prepared egg mixture evenly over the vegetables, allowing it to seep through the layers.
Grate the remaining half of the cheese and sprinkle generously over the top of the casserole.
Preheat oven to 360°F (180°C). Place the casserole in the oven and bake for 30 minutes until the top is golden brown and bubbly.
While the casserole bakes, halve 6 cherry tomatoes and finely chop the green onions for garnish.
Remove the casserole from the oven and let it cool slightly for 5-10 minutes. Cut into 12 portions.
Place a cherry tomato half on each serving and sprinkle with chopped green onions. Serve warm and enjoy!

nutrition.key_nutrients: High in protein, vitamin C, iron, calcium, and antioxidants including lycopene from tomatoes and anthocyanins from eggplant

recipe.storage: Store leftovers in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 4 days. Reheat individual portions in the microwave for 2-3 minutes or in a 350°F (175°C) oven for 15-20 minutes until heated through. This casserole can also be frozen for up to 3 months. Thaw overnight in the refrigerator before reheating.
recipe.serving_suggestions: This hearty casserole is a complete meal on its own. For a lighter accompaniment, serve with a crisp green salad dressed with lemon vinaigrette or a side of crusty bread. It pairs wonderfully with a glass of red wine. Perfect for family dinners, potlucks, or meal prep for the week. The casserole can be assembled ahead of time and refrigerated unbaked for up to 24 hours before baking.
